pois.name: Frauenkirche Dresden
pois.description: A symbol of reconciliation, this Baroque masterpiece with its unique sandstone dome was reconstructed using original stones after being destroyed in 1945.

pois.name: Dresdner Zwinger
pois.description: A magnificent palatial complex in Dresden featuring galleries, gardens, and fountains, representing the height of Saxon Baroque architecture.

pois.name: Residenzschloss Dresden
pois.description: The former residence of the Saxon Electors and Kings, now a cultural museum complex housing the famous Green Vault and Armory.

pois.name: Blaues Wunder
pois.description: A cantilever truss bridge over the Elbe in Dresden, famous for its blue paint and revolutionary engineering design at the end of the 19th century.

pois.name: Pfunds Molkerei
pois.description: Established in 1880, this historic dairy shop in Dresden is famous for its elaborate hand-painted neo-Renaissance tiles by Villeroy & Boch.

pois.name: Schloss Pillnitz
pois.description: A restored Baroque palace on the bank of the Elbe, blending Chinoiserie and Baroque styles with extensive botanical gardens.

pois.name: Völkerschlachtdenkmal
pois.description: Standing 91 meters high, this monumental structure commemorates the 1813 defeat of Napoleon at Leipzig, one of the largest war memorials in Europe.

pois.name: Thomaskirche Leipzig
pois.description: The workplace of Johann Sebastian Bach for 27 years and the home of the world-famous Thomanerchor boy choir.

pois.name: Nikolaikirche Leipzig
pois.description: The largest church in Leipzig, central to the peaceful demonstrations in 1989 that eventually led to German reunification.

pois.name: Auerbachs Keller
pois.description: Leipzig's most famous historic restaurant, immortalized as a setting in Johann Wolfgang von Goethe's play 'Faust'.

pois.name: Albrechtsburg Meißen
pois.description: Regarded as the first residential castle in German architectural history, it overlooks the Elbe and was the first site of the Meissen porcelain manufactory.

pois.name: Meißner Dom
pois.description: A prominent Gothic cathedral sharing the castle hill with Albrechtsburg, known for its ornate interior and burial place of the Wettin dynasty.

pois.name: Schloss Moritzburg
pois.description: A picturesque Baroque hunting lodge situated on an artificial island, famous for its four round towers and unique feather room collection.

pois.name: Festung Königstein
pois.description: One of the largest hilltop fortifications in Europe, situated on a table mountain in Saxon Switzerland with panoramic views and a 152-meter deep well.

pois.name: Basteibrücke
pois.description: A sandstone bridge spanning deep ravines in the Elbe Sandstone Mountains, originally built for tourism to provide views of the Neurathen rock castle ruins.

pois.name: Lilienstein
pois.description: The most prominent table mountain in the Saxon Switzerland National Park, once topped by a small medieval fortification.

pois.name: Kuhstall
pois.description: A massive natural stone arch in the Elbe Sandstone Mountains, historically used as a shelter for livestock during wartime.

pois.name: Lichtenhainer Wasserfall
pois.description: A managed waterfall in the Kirnitzsch valley, which has been a popular tourist attraction since the romantic era through controlled water releases.

pois.name: Burg Stolpen
pois.description: A medieval fortress built on basalt columns, famously known as the place where Countess Cosel, mistress of Augustus the Strong, was imprisoned for 49 years.

pois.name: Schloss Weesenstein
pois.description: A unique castle where the floors were built from top to bottom into the rock, with stables located on the fifth floor.

pois.name: Pirna Canalettoblick
pois.description: A scenic viewpoint on the Elbe River from which the painter Bernardo Bellotto, known as Canaletto, created his famous cityscapes of Pirna.

pois.name: Schloss Kriebstein
pois.description: Perched on a steep rock above the Zschopau River, this is often cited as Saxony's most beautiful knight's castle with well-preserved Gothic interiors.

pois.name: Burg Mildenstein
pois.description: One of the oldest castles in Saxony, overlooking the Freiberger Mulde river with massive Romanesque chapel and residential towers.

pois.name: Schloss Colditz
pois.description: A Renaissance castle that gained international fame during WWII as Oflag IV-C, a high-security prison for Allied officers who attempted numerous escapes.

pois.name: Schloss Hartenfels Torgau
pois.description: A major Early Renaissance castle featuring the famous Wendelstein spiral staircase and a bears' moat, serving as a center of the Reformation.

pois.name: Schloss Rochlitz
pois.description: Characterized by its two massive towers known as 'Juwen' and 'Helle', this fortress was a residence for various Wettin margraves and duchesses.

pois.name: Schloss Freudenstein
pois.description: Located in Freiberg, this Renaissance castle now houses the 'terra mineralia', one of the largest private mineral collections in the world.

pois.name: Freiberger Dom
pois.description: A Gothic cathedral famous for its 'Golden Gate' portal, the Tulip Pulpit, and the monumental Silbermann organ.

pois.name: St. Annenkirche Annaberg
pois.description: A prime example of Late Gothic architecture in the Ore Mountains, built during the mining boom with an impressive net vault and mountain altar.

pois.name: Frohnauer Hammer
pois.description: The oldest forge museum in Germany, showcasing water-powered hammer technology and preserving the industrial heritage of the Ore Mountain mining region.

pois.name: Schloss Augustusburg
pois.description: Known as the 'Crown of the Ore Mountains', this massive hunting lodge features a famous altar piece by Cranach the Younger and a large motorcycle museum.

pois.name: Schloss Wildeck
pois.description: A Zschopau landmark, this castle serves as a museum for the history of the DKW and MZ motorcycle manufacturers.

pois.name: Schloss Wolkenstein
pois.description: Perched on a high cliff above the Zschopau valley, this castle features a late Gothic design and offers historical insights into castle life.

pois.name: Brauerei Rechenberg
pois.description: One of the oldest breweries in Saxony, maintaining a historic brewhouse museum and traditional brewing techniques in the Ore Mountains.

pois.name: Erzgebirgisches Spielzeugmuseum Seiffen
pois.description: A specialized museum dedicated to the traditional wooden toy-making industry of the Ore Mountains, including nutcrackers and smoking men.

pois.name: Greifensteine
pois.description: A distinct granite rock formation in the Geyer Forest, serving as a natural climbing area and hosting a popular open-air theater.

pois.name: Fichtelberg
pois.description: The highest mountain in Saxony at 1,215 meters, offering expansive views and accessible via Germany's oldest cable car in Oberwiesenthal.

pois.name: St. Marienkirche Marienberg
pois.description: The youngest of the three large mountain churches in the Erzgebirge, representing a transition from Late Gothic to Renaissance styles.

pois.name: Karl-Marx-Monument
pois.description: A massive bronze head of Karl Marx in Chemnitz, which was known as Karl-Marx-Stadt from 1953 to 1990; it is the second-largest bust in the world.

pois.name: Roter Turm Chemnitz
pois.description: The oldest landmark of Chemnitz, this red brick tower was part of the medieval city fortifications and later served as a prison.

pois.name: Marienkirche Zwickau
pois.description: Also known as Zwickau Cathedral, this Hall church features a Baroque tower and a rich interior including a winged altar by Michael Wolgemut.

pois.name: Schloss Voigtsberg
pois.description: A hilltop castle in the Vogtland region, housing a carpet museum and mineral collection while preserving medieval fortification walls.

pois.name: Drachenhöhle Syrau
pois.description: The only show cave in Saxony, discovered by chance in 1928, featuring impressive stalactites and subterranean lakes.

pois.name: Göltzschtalbrücke
pois.description: The largest brick-built bridge in the world, consisting of over 26 million bricks and spanning the Göltzsch valley to support the railway line.

pois.name: Altes Rathaus Plauen
pois.description: The heart of Plauen's historic district, featuring a Renaissance gable and an astronomical clock added in the 16th century.

pois.name: Elstertalbrücke Pirk
pois.description: A massive stone arch motorway bridge that remained unfinished for decades after WWII, finally completed in the 1990s.

pois.name: Talsperre Eibenstock
pois.description: The largest drinking water reservoir in Saxony by surface area, created by damming the Zwickauer Mulde river.

pois.name: Görlitz Altstadt
pois.description: Germany's easternmost city, widely considered one of the best-preserved architectural heritage sites in Central Europe with Renaissance and Gothic houses.

pois.name: Heiliges Grab Görlitz
pois.description: A detailed 15th-century replica of the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem, considered one of the most faithful medieval copies remaining.

pois.name: Alte Wasserkunst
pois.description: An iconic stone water tower in Bautzen that served as part of the city fortifications and as a technical facility to pump water from the Spree.

pois.name: Kloster St. Marienstern
pois.description: An active Cistercian nunnery in Upper Lusatia, preserved in its Baroque and Gothic styles, serving as a cultural center for the Sorbian minority.

pois.name: Kloster St. Marienthal
pois.description: The oldest Cistercian nunnery in Germany that has been continuously inhabited, located directly on the Neisse river border with Poland.

pois.name: Fürst-Pückler-Park Bad Muskau
pois.description: A UNESCO World Heritage landscape garden created by Prince Pückler-Muskau, spanning the German-Polish border with a central neo-Renaissance castle.

pois.name: Rakotzbrücke
pois.description: Known as the Devil's Bridge, this basalt structure in Kromlau Park forms a perfect circle with its reflection in the water below.

pois.name: Herrnhut
pois.description: Founding place of the Moravian Brethren (Herrnhuter Brüdergemeine), known globally for the Herrnhut star and its unique Baroque town planning.

pois.name: Burg und Kloster Oybin
pois.description: Picturesque ruins of a medieval castle and Celestine monastery atop a sandstone massif, a favorite subject for Romantic painters like Caspar David Friedrich.

pois.name: Dorfkirche Cunewalde
pois.description: The largest village church in Germany, capable of seating over 2,600 people, located in a valley famous for its traditional timber-framed houses.

pois.name: Schloss Rammenau
pois.description: One of the most beautiful rural Baroque castles in Saxony, featuring unique interior wall paintings and a landscape garden.

pois.name: Kloster Altzella
pois.description: The ruins of a Cistercian monastery near Nossen, once the family monastery of the Wettin dynasty, set within a romantic 19th-century landscape park.

pois.name: Schloss Wackerbarth
pois.description: A Baroque castle and vineyard in Radebeul serving as the Saxon State Winery, famous for its sparkling wine and terraced vineyards.
